task: AccessCheck.all_allowed() should return True only if every user in the list can access the resource in access_check.py.
known_issue: Both branches return inside the first iteration, so the function only ever checks users[0] and returns its result — the loop never advances. A group where the first user is allowed but a later one is not is wrongly reported as fully allowed (a privilege check that ignores everyone after the first person).
expected_correct_fix: Return False on the first disallowed user and True only after the loop: for user in users: if not self.can_access(...): return False; return True. (Equivalent to all(...).)
